name: Family possessions, rich
description: A 3d6 biography table of wealthy household rights, obligations, inherited expectations, and claims that do not overlap with item rewards.

results.description: Revenues that should have been substantial but arrived burdened by delay, excuses, and local resistance.
A disputed inheritance important enough to keep relatives attentive and polite.
Rights over tenants, dues, or service that gave the household influence and constant irritation in equal measure.
A claim over milling, fishing, timber, toll, or grazing rights that every generation had to defend anew.
A family name that opened doors before your character had any chance to deserve it.
An expectation of advantageous marriage treated as carefully as land and discussed even more often.
Dependents, clients, or lesser households whose loyalty enlarged your family's reach and its expense.
Rights of precedence, audience, or seating that mattered socially far beyond their practical value.
Credit so easily extended to the household that others mistook it for virtue.
The obligation to maintain a larger establishment than comfort required and prudence advised.
A legal claim to revenues, offices, or protections that made the family hard to ignore and harder to love.
The expectation that your household would be consulted in local affairs whether or not it deserved the wisdom implied.
A remembered family scandal woven so tightly into the inheritance that even the benefits arrived with commentary.
Household authority over land, rents, or dependents substantial enough to shape the choices of other families.
A web of obligation, influence, and expectation that made your inheritance feel less like ownership than command.
The kind of inherited position that carried claims, prestige, duties, resentments, and the dangerous assumption that your future was already secured.
